description: Add E2E tests for critical security protections and server hardening: DNS rebinding protection (Host header validation), CORS/Origin protection, per-plugin concurrency limit enforcement, extension disconnect during ask flow, rate limiting on /reload and /mcp session creation, and unauthenticated vs authenticated /health response shapes.

userStories.title: E2E test: DNS rebinding protection rejects non-localhost Host headers
userStories.description: Test that the MCP server rejects HTTP requests with non-localhost Host headers with 403, protecting against DNS rebinding attacks from malicious websites.
acceptanceCriteria: Request with Host: evil.com returns 403 with 'Forbidden: invalid Host header'
Request with Host: localhost.evil.com returns 403
Request with Host: localhost succeeds (200 or 401 depending on auth)
Request with Host: 127.0.0.1:PORT succeeds
Request with Host: [::1]:PORT succeeds

userStories.title: E2E test: CORS protection rejects browser Origin headers
userStories.description: Test that the MCP server rejects requests with non-chrome-extension Origin headers with 403, protecting against cross-origin attacks from malicious websites.
acceptanceCriteria: Request with Origin: https://evil.com returns 403
Request with Origin: http://localhost:3000 returns 403
Request with Origin: chrome-extension://abcdef succeeds (200 or 401)
Request with no Origin header succeeds (MCP clients are not browsers)

userStories.title: E2E test: per-plugin concurrency limit rejects 6th concurrent dispatch
userStories.description: Test that the MCP server enforces the per-plugin concurrent dispatch limit of 5, rejecting the 6th concurrent call with an appropriate error message.
acceptanceCriteria: 5 concurrent tool calls to the same plugin all succeed
6th concurrent call returns error containing 'Too many concurrent dispatches'
After one of the 5 completes, a new call succeeds (slot freed)
Concurrent calls to different plugins do not interfere with each other

userStories.title: E2E test: extension disconnect during pending ask confirmation rejects MCP call
userStories.description: Test that when the extension disconnects while a confirmation dialog is pending (user has not clicked Allow/Deny), the MCP client receives a clean error rather than hanging until timeout.
acceptanceCriteria: Tool call with 'ask' permission triggers confirmation request to extension
Extension is disabled/disconnected before user responds
MCP client receives an error containing 'extension disconnected' or similar
The error arrives quickly (not after 30s timeout)
After reconnecting, the system recovers and new calls work

userStories.title: E2E test: rate limiting on POST /reload endpoint
userStories.description: Test that the POST /reload endpoint enforces rate limiting, returning 429 after exceeding the limit.
acceptanceCriteria: First 10 POST /reload requests within 60s succeed (200)
11th request returns 429 Too Many Requests
Response includes Retry-After header

userStories.title: E2E test: unauthenticated /health returns minimal response
userStories.description: Test that GET /health without a Bearer token returns only { status: 'ok' } with version header, while authenticated requests return the full response with plugin details.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /health without auth returns 200 with { status: 'ok' }
Response does NOT include plugins, pluginDetails, or auditSummary
Response includes X-Server-Version header
GET /health with valid Bearer returns 200 with full response including plugins field
GET /health with invalid Bearer returns 401

userStories.title: E2E test: network capture cleanup when tab closes
userStories.description: Test that when network capture is active on a tab and the tab is closed, the capture session is cleaned up and does not block future captures.
acceptanceCriteria: Enable network capture on a tab via browser_enable_network_capture
Close the tab
extension_get_state shows no active captures for the closed tab
Enabling network capture on a new tab succeeds (not blocked by stale state)
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/security.e2e.ts (same file)

Context:
Network capture uses Chrome's debugger API. When a tab closes, the debugger session is automatically detached by Chrome. The extension needs to clean up its internal `activeCaptures` map.

`extension-commands.ts` line 77-93 includes `networkCaptures` in the state response from `extension_get_state`.

Test approach:
1. Open a tab
2. Call `browser_enable_network_capture` with the tab's ID
3. Verify capture is active (call `extension_get_state` or `browser_get_network_requests`)
4. Close the tab
5. Call `extension_get_state` — verify no active captures for that tab ID
6. Open a new tab, enable capture — should succeed

Constraints:
- Need 'auto' permission for browser tools.
- The tab ID must be discovered via `browser_list_tabs` or known from the open step.

userStories.title: E2E test: config mutex serializes concurrent permission writes
userStories.description: Test that multiple concurrent permission changes are serialized correctly via the config write mutex, with all changes persisted without data loss.
acceptanceCriteria: 5 concurrent setToolPermission calls for different tools all succeed
After all complete, config.json reflects all 5 permission changes
No changes are lost or overwritten by concurrent writes
